Gold.data_anomaly: Gold price $3980.50 is outside expected range ($4000-$7000). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Monday's session saw the Dow close above 52,000 for the first time, with tech leading a broad rally after a weekend US-Iran ceasefire halted the latest Hormuz flare-up. SPY gained 1.3%, QQQ surged 2.5%, and SOXX bounced 4.5% from Friday's rout. The VIX eased to 17.65, but the S&P 500 remains below its 50-day moving average — a warning signal for the broader market ahead of Thursday's non-farm payrolls.
commodities_note: WTI held $70.60, nearly flat as Hormuz risk premium unwinds. Gold slumped 2.5% to $3980.5 (data anomaly flagged — likely a contract discrepancy; verify source). US10Y steady at 4.37%. DXY -0.07%. Gold's move suggests safe-haven unwinding.

writeup.headline: WATCH — MEMS timing thesis real, but 66x PE blocks accumulate.
writeup.what_to_do: SiTime's MEMS timing displacement of quartz is a structural AI infrastructure play, but the stock is only +4.9% above the SMA50 with a 1.8:1 R:R and 66x forward PE. The entry is borderline; a pullback to $677.44 SMA50 would create a cleaner setup. The watchlist addition is high-conviction, but the valuation requires patience — no entry until RSI dips below 45 or support at $657.38 breaks on volume.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 66x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if SITM reverts to HOLD on the next session — the signal upgrade requires confirmation.

singapore.summary: All three banks extended above their 50-DMAs, but NIM headwinds from US10Y above 4.20% and MAS S$NEER appreciation persist. DBS and OCBC upgraded to WATCH on technical improvement, while UOB remains hard-blocked. The key differentiator is UOB's cheap valuation at 10.9x PE, but the extension blocks entry.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally constrained — NIM compression persists, but price strength adds monitoring.
singapore.key_development: US10Y held at 4.37%, well above the 4.20% threshold, extending NIM pressure; no relief until October MAS.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster bounced 4.5% Monday, with SOXX outpacing the broad market. Memory names (MU, Hynix) remain the strongest on earnings, but extreme extensions block entry across the board. The divergence between fundamentally cheap (NVDA PEG 0.59, MU PE 7.7x) and technically extended underscores the discipline required: great thesis, no entry.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — memory leads, but entry blocked by extreme extension.
semiconductors.key_development: MU Q3 blowout ($25.11 vs $20.71) and Citi's $2,500 SNDK PT kept the memory upcycle narrative alive, but the cluster's 8 hard-block names keep new capital on the sidelines.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverged sharply: GOOG's Dow inclusion and TPU strength sent it +4.5%, while MSFT fell another 1.1% to its worst month since 2000 on AI demand concerns. The divergence reflects a market pricing in GOOG's in-house AI advantage vs MSFT's OpenAI dependency. MSFT is the lone ACCUMULATE, with Azure non-OpenAI RPO the clean read.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but credit-circularity risk remains; MSFT's ACCUMULATE is contingent on 7/30 earnings.
big_tech.key_development: GOOG joined the Dow, triggering passive buying; MSFT continued to underperform on OpenAI IPO delay and capex ROI doubts.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment includes both LITE and COHR — the AI optical interconnect supply chain is consolidating.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 10% from current levels, aligning with NVDA's optical networking catalyst.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and optical networking exposure links directly to AVGO, NVDA, and hyperscaler capex themes.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AVGO earnings for custom ASIC demand read; potential entry on 15% pullback.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: SK Hynix's HBM competitor — $576B Korean chip investment and DRAM output doubling directly impact memory supply dynamics.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if HBM3E qualification news emerges, tightening the duopoly further.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking share gainer, benefiting from NVDA Spectrum-X and hyperscaler capex.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch for post-earnings pullback to 200-DMA; AI networking demand is structural but currently priced in.

active_risks: US-Iran ceasefire after weekend strikes calms Hormuz, but risk of re-escalation remains high; WTI back to $70.60.
China imposes export controls on 40 Japanese entities, widening the tech decoupling beyond semiconductors to drones and nuclear.
Fed hawkish pause under Chair Warsh with PCE at 4.1% keeps rate hike risk alive; payrolls Thursday will refine expectations.
US-China rare earth and defense blacklist tensions persist, with Alibaba lawsuit ongoing and BYD also targeted.
geopolitical.market_impact: The ceasefire and tech rally lifted SOXX +4.5%, but the S&P 500 remains below its 50-DMA, reflecting lingering rate and growth concerns. Oil stability benefits transport, while gold's -2.5% drop unwinds safe-haven flows. The Singapore banks remain under NIM compression as MAS and US10Y stay tight.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: China imposes export controls on 40 Japanese entities, targeting drone makers and nuclear firms, expanding the US-led tech war front.

base.description: Hormuz ceasefire holds, WTI stabilizes near $70. Fed holds rates, S&P grinds sideways below 50-DMA. Semis stay extended, BigTech diverges, with MSFT ACCUMULATE the only active entry.

optimistic.description: US-Iran peace deal materializes, oil dips below $65. Payrolls weaken, easing rate hike fears, tech rallies. GOOG and NVDA reclaim SMA50, upgrade to BUY; semis cluster compression becomes entry opportunity.

pessimistic.description: Iran re-strikes, WTI spikes above $90. Payrolls strong, reinforcing hawkish Fed; Nasdaq drops 5%. Semis sell off sharply, MSFT accumulation invalidated as VIX surges above 30.

wildcard.description: China-Japan tech war escalation disrupts key semiconductor materials, hitting ASML and TSM. The S&P 500 falls below 200-DMA, forcing broad CAUTION/AVOID across the watchlist.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Maintain defensive posture; accumulate MSFT starter at current levels, but defer other entries until payrolls and oil stabilize. Avoid extended semis.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 8 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension — extreme semis keep entry doors closed despite compelling fundamentals; MSFT is the sole ACCUMULATE, banking on Azure's non-OpenAI demand.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=3) / ACCUMULATE (n=4) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
